signal_candidates.claim: Blockratize, Inc. d/b/a Polymarket was ordered to pay a $1.4 million civil monetary penalty by the CFTC for offering off-exchange event-based binary options contracts and failure to obtain designation as a designated contract market (DCM) or registration as a swap execution facility (SEF).
signal_candidates.reason: The document states that the CFTC entered an order filing and simultaneously settling charges against Blockratize, Inc. d/b/a Polymarket for offering off-exchange event-based binary options contracts and failure to obtain designation as a DCM or registration as a SEF. This indicates regulatory scrutiny and potential risk to the company's risk rating.
signal_candidates.verbatim_evidence: The order requires that Polymarket pay a $1.4 million civil monetary penalty, facilitate the resolution (i.e. wind down) of all markets displayed on Polymarket.com that do not comply with the Commodity Exchange Act (CEA) and applicable CFTC regulations, and cease and desist from violating the CEA and CFTC regulations, as charged.
